title: cli-flags: use a consistent format for <size><unit>
body:
Use a consistent format for description of the <size><unit> flags.
Also, avoid backticks for /dev/shm, as that's interpreted as the format
by the flag parsing lib.

Signed-off-by: Marco Vedovati <mvedovati@suse.com>

------------------------

Before / after:
```
$ podman run --help | grep unit
      --kernel-memory <number>[<unit>]           Kernel memory limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
  -m, --memory string                            Memory limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
      --memory-reservation string                Memory soft limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
      --shm-size /dev/shm                        Size of /dev/shm. The format is `<number><unit>` (default "65536k")

$ ./bin/podman run --help | grep unit
      --kernel-memory <number>[<unit>]           Kernel memory limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
  -m, --memory <number>[<unit>]                  Memory limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
      --memory-reservation <number>[<unit>]      Memory soft limit (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g)
      --shm-size <number>[<unit>]                Size of /dev/shm (format: <number>[<unit>], where unit = b, k, m or g) (default "65536k")

```

comments.body: This is hung up on a remote test.  @mheon did you get a chance to look at the shm-size==0 question?
